concerns.text: Task T010 instructs implementing 'Google Earth Engine authentication setup... to enable API access for CI reproducibility'. This violates Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) which mandates results be reproducible on a fresh runner without manual intervention. Interactive authentication (earthengine authenticate) is non-reproducible in CI. The task must be revised to use a service account or token-based auth mechanism compatible with CI, not the interactive setup command.

Task T028 describes sweeping regularization parameters over 'a range of small positive values'. This silently relaxes FR-006 and SC-003 which explicitly mandate the discrete set {0.01, 0.05, 0.1}. A continuous or undefined range changes the validation scope and makes the acceptance criterion (SC-003) untestable as written.

Task T029 instructs measuring importance by 'permuted versus excluded (zeroed)'. This conflates Permutation Importance (FR-007, SC-004) with Feature Ablation. The spec explicitly requires permutation importance. Mixing methods or using zeroing instead of permutation violates the specific metric definition in the success criteria.

Task T023 specifies 'Temporal Holdout (train -2021, test 2022-2023)'. The syntax 'train -2021' is ambiguous and potentially excludes 2021 data, contradicting the Plan's explicit 'train 2018-2021' and FR-001's 2018-2023 range. This ambiguity risks implementing a different temporal split than the plan, violating the validation strategy.

Task T024a requires a baseline using 'only temperature data' but fails to specify if this is daily or aggregated. FR-002 and US-1 define the input as temperature, precipitation, and solar radiation aligned to 10-day intervals. Without specifying the aggregation level (e.g., 10-day mean temp), the baseline may not be comparable to the main model's feature set, violating SC-001's requirement for a valid comparison.
